sources.title: Fecal metabolite profiling identifies liver transplant recipients at risk for postoperative infection

data_repositories.evidence: Repository README states it contains code and data for the manuscript and includes Data and Results folders.
Repository README links to Zenodo as the zipped data download option.

sources.reported_sample_scope: 107 liver transplantation patients with fecal metabolites correlated to microbiome composition, pathobiont expansion, and postoperative infection.
database_value: Adds a true non-transcriptomic liver transplant evidence layer.
Connects fecal metabolite concentrations, microbiome dysbiosis, and clinical infection outcomes.
Supports gut-liver axis and infection-risk use cases that transcriptomics alone cannot answer.
next_ingest_steps: Normalize metabolite feature labels to HMDB, ChEBI, and PubChem identifiers where available.
Normalize MetaPhlAn lineage labels to NCBI taxonomy identifiers.
Reproduce or expose the manuscript's multivariable infection-risk models after feature identity normalization.
Add per-sample feature matrices or controlled-size slices for downloadable analysis workflows.

sources.title: Serum metabolomics for liver graft pathology prediction

data_repositories.evidence: The article text states that Supplementary Table S4 contains absolute metabolite concentrations for each sample.
Public supplementary ZIP contains Table S4.xlsx together with figures and methods.

sources.reported_sample_scope: 55 post-liver-transplant serum samples spanning biopsy-confirmed TCMR, biliary complications, and post-transplant MASH.
database_value: Adds a direct post-transplant serum metabolomics evidence layer rather than a reference atlas.
Supports non-invasive discrimination of TCMR, biliary complications, and metabolic graft injury.
Exposes public absolute concentration values that can be queried metabolite-by-metabolite.
next_ingest_steps: Normalize metabolite names to HMDB, ChEBI, and PubChem identifiers where possible.
Link top metabolites to the paper's machine-learning ranking and public clinical covariates if reusable.
Validate serum metabolite shifts in an independent liver transplant cohort before claiming classifier-level generalization.

sources.title: Human liver cells proteome

data_repositories.evidence: PRIDE project metadata describes mass spectrometry proteomic analysis of human liver cell compartments.

sources.reported_sample_scope: Reference human liver cell proteome; not a liver transplantation cohort.
database_value: Provides protein-level context for hepatocyte and non-parenchymal liver cell programs.
Useful as a reference layer when transplant-specific proteomics is sparse.
next_ingest_steps: Map all UniProt accessions to reviewed canonical identifiers where possible.
Add protein feature pages that cross-link RNA expression, single-cell markers, and protein reference compartments.
Keep all PXD012615 evidence marked as liver reference, not transplant direct evidence.

sources.title: Serum proteomic biomarkers for acute rejection and biliary complications after liver transplantation

data_repositories.evidence: The full-text article identifies ACLY, FGA, and APOA1 as liver-transplant serum proteomic biomarkers and links peaks to proteins in Table 1.
Supplementary Tables 2-4 provide published group mean plus SD peak intensities for perioperative, acute rejection, and ITBL comparisons.

sources.reported_sample_scope: Direct liver-transplant recipient serum proteomics biomarker cohort reconstructed from published MALDI-TOF peak summaries; contrasts are available for stable post-transplant versus acute rejection, stable post-transplant versus ITBL, and perioperative pre/post-transplant effectiveness contexts.
database_value: Adds a transplant-specific proteomics layer instead of only liver-cell reference proteomics.
Supports non-invasive graft monitoring by exposing ACLY, FGA, and APOA1 serum biomarker evidence across rejection and biliary-complication contexts.
Provides direct post-transplant protein evidence that can be interpreted alongside blood RNA and serum metabolomics layers.
next_ingest_steps: Keep this source labeled as published group-summary serum biomarker evidence rather than a full per-sample proteomics matrix.
Cross-link ACLY, FGA, and APOA1 with blood transcriptomic and serum metabolomic evidence in non-invasive monitoring views.
Replace with a richer direct proteomics cohort if a public per-sample transplant proteome matrix becomes reusable.

sources.title: Liver graft proteomics reveals potential incipient mechanisms behind early renal dysfunction after liver transplantation

data_repositories.evidence: The full article reports postreperfusion liver graft proteomics comparing recipients with moderate/severe early AKI versus recipients without AKI after liver transplantation.
Table 2 in the public PDF lists differential proteins with UniProt accession, gene symbol, fold change, and p value.

sources.reported_sample_scope: Direct liver-transplant graft-tissue proteomics from postreperfusion biopsies, exposed as a published differential protein table for moderate/severe early AKI versus no early AKI.
database_value: Adds direct graft-tissue transplant proteomics instead of only serum biomarker evidence or liver reference proteomes.
Supports injury-context interpretation with inflammatory, endothelial, protease, and lipoprotein signals such as SAA2, PLA2G2A, PECAM1, and APOA1.
Provides a tissue proteomics bridge between graft transcriptomics and blood-based multimodal monitoring layers.
next_ingest_steps: Keep this source framed as published differential-table tissue proteomics rather than a per-sample intensity matrix.
Use it to contextualize injury-versus-rejection interpretation, especially for innate inflammation and endothelial activation markers.
Replace or extend it if a reusable transplant proteomics matrix with sample-level abundances becomes public.

sources.title: Metabolomic and proteomic analyses of renal function after liver transplantation

data_repositories.evidence: The article body exposes Table 3 with 45 differential serum proteins separating Normal-KF from Impaired-KF after liver transplantation, plus Table 4 Reactome pathway annotations.

The article links the direct post-transplant LC-MS/MS cohort to PRIDE accession PXD062924, confirming a real public repository record even though this V1 layer uses the published article table rather than a reusable per-sample matrix.

sources.reported_sample_scope: Direct liver-transplant recipient serum proteomics cohort with week-2 and week-5 post-transplant sampling, exposed in V1 as a published differential protein table for Normal-KF versus Impaired-KF plus a separately described recovered patient.
database_value: Adds a direct post-transplant serum proteomics layer tied to renal dysfunction rather than only rejection or biliary complication evidence.
Extends blood-based monitoring with proteins such as B2M, AMBP, TF, APOA1, and SLPI that can be interpreted alongside blood RNA and serum metabolomics layers.
Creates a bridge between transplant complication monitoring and PRIDE-linked proteomics provenance without overstating access to a full sample-by-protein matrix.
next_ingest_steps: Keep this source framed as published serum differential-table evidence rather than a reusable per-sample proteomics matrix.
Cross-link renal-dysfunction proteins such as B2M and AMBP with blood monitoring views without labeling them as rejection-specific biomarkers.
If a reusable processed intensity matrix becomes accessible from PRIDE or supplementary files, upgrade this source from article-table evidence to sample-level proteomics.
